title: McGraw-Hill's Core Data Structures
description: A hierarchical flowchart illustrating the categorization and relationships between fundamental data structures as presented in McGraw-Hill's educational material.
elements: Primitive Data Structures (int, float, char, etc.)
relationships: Primitive structures form the building blocks for all others
Linear and Non-Linear are the two main categories derived from primitives
Arrays and Linked Lists are subtypes of Linear structures
Stacks and Queues are abstract data types often implemented using Arrays or Linked Lists
Trees and Graphs are subtypes of Non-Linear structures
Hash Tables are a separate, high-level implementation that often uses Arrays
